title: Fermented French Lamb Shanks Stir-fry
description: A silky and fermented vegan-friendly dish featuring lamb shanks, perfectly suitable for meal-prep.
instructions: Sprinkle baking soda over the lamb shanks. Place in a baking dish.
Mix soy sauce and olive oil together, then pour over the lamb shanks, ensuring they are coated evenly.
Bake for about an hour until the lamb is well done (internal temperature of 145°F/63°C).
While lamb is baking, sauté leeks, onion, and bell pepper in olive oil until softened.
Stir-fry minced fermented vegetables if using. Add to the sautéed veggies and mix thoroughly.
Remove lamb shanks from oven, let them rest for a few minutes, then slice into bite-sized pieces.
Gently reheat sliced lamb on medium heat with the stir-fried veg mixture for about 5 minutes, allowing flavors to blend together.
Serve hot over rice or noodles as desired.
